name: El Rallar - Arangoiti – circular from Leire
name_original: El Rallar - Arangoiti – circular desde Leire
region_intro: Hiking around Yesa offers diverse terrain in the foothills of the Pyrenees, characterized by rolling hills, dense woodlands, and the expansive Lac de Yesa. The region features varied elevations, including the prominent Arangoiti hills, providing both gentle paths and more challenging ascents. Yesa hiking trails often lead through areas with historical sites and offer views over the reservoir. Best hiking trails around Yesa The most popular hiking route is Aita Santuaren Ibilbidea - Peña del Adiós Trail, an 8.4 miles (13.5 km) trail that takes 3 hours 47 minutes to complete. This moderate route features varied terrain and views. Another top favourite among local hikers is El Rallar - Arangoiti – circular from Leire, a moderate 5.8 miles (9.3 km) path. This trail offers panoramic views of the Yesa reservoir and the Arangoiti hills. Local hikers also love the Leyre Monastery – View from El Rallar (1,282 m) loop from Monasterio de Leire, a 4.2 miles (6.8 km) trail leading through forested slopes with views towards the monastery and Lac de Yesa, often completed in about 2 hours 42 minutes. Hiking around Yesa is defined by reservoir views, forested hills, and paths connecting historical sites. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy hikes near Yesa to more challenging routes. The routes in Yesa are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 300 reviews. More than 2000 hikers have used komoot to explore Yesa's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Yesa?
A: Yesa offers a diverse network of over 60 hiking trails. These routes traverse varied terrain, from the rolling hills and dense woodlands to paths along the expansive Lac de Yesa, catering to different skill levels.

Q: Are there easy hiking options for beginners or families in Yesa?
A: Yes, Yesa has around 12 easy hiking trails perfect for beginners or families. These routes typically feature gentle paths through woodlands or along the reservoir, offering pleasant walks without significant elevation changes. While specific family-friendly routes aren't highlighted in the data, the easier trails are generally suitable.

Q: What kind of challenging hikes can I find near Yesa?
A: For those seeking a challenge, Yesa features about 14 difficult hiking trails. These routes often involve more significant ascents into the Arangoiti hills, providing strenuous climbs and rewarding panoramic views over the region and Lac de Yesa.

Q: Are there any circular walks around Yesa?
A: Yes, many of the trails around Yesa are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. An excellent example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e966276677/el-rallar-arangoiti-circular-from-leire" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Rallar - Arangoiti – circular from Leire&lt;/a&gt;, which offers panoramic views of the reservoir and hills. Another popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17681143" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Leyre Monastery – View from El Rallar (1,282 m) loop from Monasterio de Leire&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are the best viewpoints along the hiking trails in Yesa?
A: Many trails in Yesa offer stunning viewpoints, particularly those ascending the Arangoiti hills or overlooking Lac de Yesa. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e966276677/el-rallar-arangoiti-circular-from-leire" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Rallar - Arangoiti – circular from Leire&lt;/a&gt; route is known for its panoramic vistas of the reservoir. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17681143" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Leyre Monastery – View from El Rallar (1,282 m) loop from Monasterio de Leire&lt;/a&gt; also provides excellent views towards the monastery and the lake.

Q: What historical sites or landmarks can I explore while hiking in Yesa?
A: The Yesa region is rich in history. You can explore sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/934414" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Xavier&lt;/a&gt;, which is part of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24645786" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of the Annunciation – Castle of Xavier loop from Javier&lt;/a&gt;. Other notable landmarks include the Leyre Monastery, visible from several trails, and the ruins of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5266483" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruesta Castle&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any gorges or natural monuments to see near Yesa?
A: Yes, the area around Yesa boasts impressive natural formations. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2933330" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Foz de Lumbier (Lumbier Gorge)&lt;/a&gt; is a prominent natural monument, known for its dramatic cliffs and as a habitat for vultures. Another significant gorge is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4099433" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Foz de Arbayun&lt;/a&gt;, and you can also find the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7575483" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Paso del Oso Natural Rock Arch&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Yesa?
A: The hiking experience in Yesa is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars. Reviewers often praise the diverse terrain, the stunning reservoir views, and the well-maintained paths that connect historical sites and natural beauty.

Q: Is it possible to hike with dogs on the trails around Yesa?
A: While specific regulations for dogs on every trail are not provided, generally, dogs are welcome on many outdoor trails in the region, especially if kept on a leash. It's always advisable to check local signage or specific trail rules before heading out with your dog, particularly in nature reserves or protected areas.

Q: Are there any wild swimming spots near Yesa's hiking trails?
A: The expansive Lac de Yesa is a prominent feature of the region, and while it's a reservoir, some areas may be suitable for swimming. However, specific designated wild swimming spots directly adjacent to hiking trails are not detailed. Always exercise caution and check local regulations regarding swimming in the reservoir.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Yesa?
A: The Yesa region, located in the foothills of the Pyrenees, generally offers pleasant hiking conditions during spring and autumn when temperatures are mild. Summer can be warm, especially at lower elevations, while winter may bring colder weather and potentially snow at higher altitudes, though many trails remain accessible.

Q: Are there options for public transport to reach hiking trailheads in Yesa?
A: While Yesa is a rural area, some regional bus services might connect to nearby towns or villages. However, direct public transport access to all trailheads can be limited. For maximum flexibility, driving and utilizing available parking near trail starts is often the most convenient option for hikers in this region.
tips: Although you can reach the top of Arangoiti by other trails, this is undoubtedly the most picturesque and colorful. Throughout the entire journey you will have amazing views of the entire Yesa valley and the reservoir of the same name. A path that deserves to travel and enjoy!
